# HN Launch Post: "Show HN: System Design Handbook — 25 Modules, 450+ Topics, 550+ Diagrams"

## Title (choose one):
1. **Show HN: I built the most comprehensive system design resource on GitHub — 25 modules, 450+ topics, 550+ Mermaid diagrams**
2. **Show HN: System Design Mastery — from zero to staff engineer, 25 modules deep**

## Body:

Over the past months I've been building what I believe is the most comprehensive open-source system design learning resource on GitHub.

It started as my personal study notes for system design interviews, but grew into something much larger as I kept adding modules. Today it covers 25 modules — from CS fundamentals all the way to staff engineer.

**What's inside:**
- 450+ topics across 25 modules
- 550+ Mermaid diagrams (every topic has at least one)
- 22 real-world architecture case studies (Netflix, Stripe, Figma, Cloudflare, TikTok, etc.)
- 7 production incident postmortems
- 26 interview problems with full solutions
- 19 hands-on projects with end-to-end implementation guides
- Multi-cloud mapping (AWS × Azure × GCP)
- Certifications path for all 3 clouds
- AI/ML System Design module (LLMs, RAG, vector databases, model serving)
- Staff Engineer module (strategy, RFCs, incident leadership)
- Clean Architecture, API Design, Testing & Quality modules

**The format is consistent across all files:**
What is it → Why it was created → When to use it → Architecture (with Mermaid) → Hands-on example → Pricing → Best practices → Interview questions → Real company usage

**Links:** https://github.com/ajju853/SystemDesignHandbook

I'd love feedback on anything — missing topics, incorrect content, areas to improve. Contributions are welcome, and there's a validation CI pipeline to make contributing easy.

**Questions I'm thinking about:**
- What topics are missing that would make this more useful?
- Is the 25-module structure intuitive, or would you organize it differently?
- Should I add video walkthroughs for key modules?
